title: Darwin Godel Machine: Open-Ended Evolution of Self-Improving Agents
authors: Jenny Zhang, Shengran Hu, Cong Lu et al. (Sakana AI)
description: First practical self-improving coding agent via Darwinian evolution + open-ended exploration; SWE-bench 20%->50%.

title: Live-Evo: Online Evolution of Agentic Memory from Continuous Feedback
description: Online self-evolving memory with Experience Bank + Meta-Guideline Bank; +20.8% Brier score on Prophet Arena. Addresses distribution shift in live settings.

title: WebEvolver: Enhancing Web Agent Self-Improvement with Co-evolving World Model
authors: Tianqing Fang et al. (Tencent AI Lab)
description: EMNLP 2025 Main. Co-evolving World Model LLM as virtual server + imagination engine; 10% gain over existing self-evolving agents without closed-source distillation.

title: EvolveR: Self-Evolving LLM Agents through an Experience-Driven Lifecycle
description: ICLR 2026. Complete closed-loop: Online Interaction -> Offline Self-Distillation -> RL evolution; distills abstract principles, not raw trajectories; no external teacher needed.

title: Dr. Zero: Self-Evolving Search Agents without Training Data
authors: Zhenrui Yue et al. (Meta Superintelligence Labs, UIUC)
description: Data-free self-evolution via proposer-solver co-evolution with HRPO; matches supervised performance with zero training data.

title: Position: Agentic Evolution is the Path to Evolving LLMs
description: Argues evolution is a new scaling axis beyond training-time and inference-time compute; proposes evolution-scaling hypothesis.
note: Position paper framing the emerging paradigm.

title: AI 2027
authors: Daniel Kokotajlo, Scott Alexander, Eli Lifland et al.
description: Most detailed public scenario forecast: AI agent automation of coding by early 2027, intelligence explosion late 2027. Nov 2025 update: revised median to around 2030 with lots of uncertainty. 1M+ readers.
note: US VP Vance read it; NYT/Metaculus/80K Hours coverage.

title: AI as Normal Technology
description: Knight First Amendment Institute, 40 pages. AI is a 'normal' GPT like electricity; highlights capability-reliability gap as fundamental barrier. 'What will 2027 look like? Pretty much like 2025.'
note: NYT, MIT Tech Review coverage; expanding to book (2027).

title: Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Software Tasks (METR)
authors: Thomas Kwa, Alexander West et al.
description: 50%-task-completion time horizon metric; exponential growth with ~7-month doubling (possibly accelerating to ~4 months); GPT-5 ~2h17min horizon.

title: Inference-Time Reward Hacking in Large Language Models
description: NeurIPS 2025 Spotlight. Characterizes reward hacking in inference-time alignment; Best-of-Poisson (BoP) as mitigation; hedging on proxy reward provably mitigates hacking.

title: School of Reward Hacks: Hacking Harmless Tasks Generalizes to Misalignment
description: Reward hacking from coding RL generalizes to broader misalignment -- alignment faking, sabotage planning. Inoculation prompting reduces by 75-90%.

title: Scaling over Scaling: Exploring Test-Time Scaling Plateau
description: Derives mathematical saturation points for parallel and sequential TTC strategies; both converge to unified upper bounds; validated on AIME, MATH-500, GPQA.

title: The Illusion of Diminishing Returns: Measuring Long Horizon Execution
description: Contrarian: diminishing returns on single-step metrics compound to large benefits in task length; thinking mitigates self-conditioning, enabling much longer tasks.

title: A Survey of Self-Evolving Agents: On Path to ASI
description: Comprehensive survey bridging foundation models and lifelong agentic systems. Also: 2508.07407 (Fang et al.).
note: GitHub: CharlesQ9/Self-Evolving-Agents. Defining survey for the field.

title: EvolMathEval: Towards Evolvable Benchmarks
description: Evolved benchmarks reduce model accuracy by avg 48%; Pseudo Aha Moment -- LLMs bypass complex reasoning via simplistic shortcuts (77-100% of errors).

title: AlphaEvolve: A Coding Agent for Scientific and Algorithmic Discovery
Evo-Memory: Benchmarking LLM Agent Test-time Learning with Self-Evolving Memory
